authoritative_sources.title: Commercial Use Authorizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NPS is the federal authority for commercial visitor services on national park lands, and its CUA guidance directly constrains whether commercial tours may operate, what activity approval is required, and when permit conditions affect route, guide, and supplier execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: tour authority verification, permit-sensitive itinerary readiness, and route or activity substitution review

authoritative_sources.title: Commercial Use Authorization Application Instructions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NPS CUA instructions identify authorization terms, operating-plan expectations, insurance conditions, and road-based commercial tour or transportation activity constraints that day-of-tour execution must not bypass.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: operator authorization, tour transport, operating-plan dependency, and commercial-tour record review
used_for: tour supplier and road-based tour evidence

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official guide defines total-price and mandatory-fee disclosure rules for live-event tickets and short-term lodging that can be included in tour packages or admission substitutions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ticket, admission, mandatory-fee, package-price, and guest-facing substitution communication
used_for: public baseline for consumer-facing price statements

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 380 - Public Charters
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes the current DOT public-charter rules, and 14 CFR Part 380 is authoritative for tour packages that include public charter flights, deposits, security agreements, cancellations, participant substitutions, price increases, refunds, and record-retention conditions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: charter-sensitive booking, cancellation, refund, and package-travel exception handling
used_for: public charter refund and price-change boundary

authoritative_sources.title: Using the Bus Safety Search: A Step-By-Step Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FMCSA is the federal motor-carrier safety regulator, and its Bus Safety Search guidance directly supports passenger-carrier authorization and safety checks before booking or using bus transport for tours.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: transport readiness, vehicle or carrier substitution, and safety-sensitive departure hold decisions
used_for: safety escalation when carrier status cannot be verified

authoritative_sources.title: Avoid Scams When You Travel
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC travel-scam guidance is authoritative public consumer-fraud guidance for suspicious travel-sale, payment, refund, and identity signals that tour operations must escalate instead of resolving in the dispatch lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fraud-sensitive transfer, duplicate voucher, suspicious refund reroute, and account-change exception handling
used_for: fraud and suspicious payment stop conditions

authoritative_sources.title: Are call center environments considered sensitive areas for PCI DSS Requirement 9.1.1?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body; this FAQ directly constrains cardholder-data handling in call-center, dispatch, or guest-service workflows connected to tour payment exceptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-data handling, refund request triage, and cardholder-data refusal conditions

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 21101: Adventure tourism - Safety management systems - Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO is a recognized standards body, and ISO 21101 is directly relevant to adventure-tourism safety management, participant-risk controls, and escalation when an activity, route, weather condition, or guide readiness changes the risk posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: participant-risk review, activity substitution, guide readiness, weather disruption, and safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Traveler Support and Complaints
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTA is a recognized travel-advisor professional association; its public consumer-affairs process clarifies supplier-first resolution boundaries and evidence required for travel-service complaints without granting tour operations refund or supplier-override authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: guest exception evidence, complaint packaging, supplier handoff, and service-recovery boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: Intake classification and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm departure, tour code, route, guest, artifact set, system access, and whether the request is tour execution, blocked, or adjacent-lane work before taking action.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Manifest, ticket, guide, transport, and supplier readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Validate manifest counts, pickup windows, ticket or voucher availability, supplier confirmations, guide assignment, transport readiness, capacity, and permit or venue constraints.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle state progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance, hold, rework, or complete the tour execution state only when authoritative records support the transition and reversal conditions are documented.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Guest-impact exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Package delays, substitutions, missed pickups, no-shows, capacity failures, accessibility-sensitive requests, and supplier misses with guest impact, evidence, and the correct next owner.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Policy, pricing, refund, fraud, safety, and permit escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop direct execution when the answer depends on refund eligibility, pricing authority, suspicious payment, unsafe transport, guide credentialing, permit terms, or public-policy conflict.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Close work only after manifest reconciliation, ticket or admission status, transport completion, guide closeout, no-show or standby disposition, and unresolved exception ownership are recorded.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

artifacts: tour manifests, departure lists, tour codes, route sheets, run-of-show versions, and pickup windows
guest booking references, party sizes, accessibility notes, standby or no-show logs, and guest exception logs
ticket allotments, will-call records, vouchers, timed-entry admissions, supplier confirmations, and venue scan reports
guide assignments, guide check-in records, credentials, licenses, briefing acknowledgments, and contact information
transport confirmations, vehicle or operator identifiers, driver details, USDOT or state safety lookup context, delay notices, and route constraints
capacity holds, minimum passenger counts, substitution approvals, permit or CUA conditions, and operating-plan references
CRM notes, guest communications, complaint histories, cancellation and refund terms, service-recovery thresholds, fraud indicators, and payment dispute context
closeout reports, manifest reconciliation, ticket reconciliation, transport completion, guide completion, incident notes, and unresolved owner handoff records
systems_of_record: transport management or charter supplier system

allowed: Classify work as in scope, out of scope, blocked on prerequisites, exception-sensitive, or adjacent-lane owned.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required for safe tour execution.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented tour execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed departure readiness summaries, guest-impact exception notes, completion records, and handoff packets.
escalate: Manifest, ticketing, supplier, transport, guide, capacity, permit, payment, or CRM records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires refund, pricing, booking change, supplier substitution, safety clearance, fraud decisioning, legal interpretation, or permit approval.
Unsafe carrier, weather, route, activity, public-health, suspicious identity, duplicate voucher, or card-data handling signals change whether execution can proceed.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, venue, supplier, transport, processor, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Mark a tour ready or complete without manifest, ticket, guide, transport, supplier, and exception evidence.
Promise a refund, complimentary transfer, admission replacement, fee waiver, private transport, or supplier substitution outside delegated authority.
Override pricing, refund, safety, permit, guide credential, fraud, or payment-security controls without explicit authority.
Ask for or restate full payment-card data in dispatch, guide, guest messaging, email, SMS, or unprotected call-center workflows.

domain_failure_modes: a departure is released despite missing ticket allotment, guide check-in, transport confirmation, capacity, or supplier evidence
the lane promises a refund, private vehicle, ticket replacement, or complimentary substitution when policy or approval records do not support it
booking, manifest, ticket, and payment records conflict but the specialist completes the work rather than routing reconciliation
a guide or route substitution bypasses permit, venue, credential, commercial-use authorization, or supplier-contract conditions
transport safety or carrier authorization cannot be verified but departure is treated as routine
duplicate voucher, suspicious transfer, identity mismatch, or refund reroute signals are handled as ordinary guest service
cardholder data is requested or repeated in an unapproved dispatch or guest communication channel
the package collapses into generic operations language instead of tour-specific manifest, ticketing, guide, transport, and run-of-show terminology
tour closeout lacks audit-ready manifest reconciliation, ticket status, guide closeout, transport completion, and unresolved exception disposition

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific tourism, guide licensing, transport, and public-land permit rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant retrieval of jurisdiction, venue, operator, guide, permit, and supplier rules before execution claims that depend on them.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific refund, substitution, service-recovery, and guest communication authority
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Use tenant authority matrices and approval thresholds at runtime; otherwise escalate rather than promise exceptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tour type variation across city sightseeing, food tours, park tours, adventure tours, and air-inclusive packages
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: The package defines a public baseline and requires tour-type-specific records and sources of truth before applying specialized execution rules.

notes: The source mix covers public tourism operating authority, fee and refund boundaries, passenger transport safety, travel fraud, payment security, adventure-tour safety, and travel complaint evidence.
The final spec is constrained to day-of-tour execution and evidence packaging; refund, pricing, fraud, safety, permit, and supplier approval decisions remain outside the lane.
